scenario_results.reason: Converted repeat incidents into a bounded problem record with explicit missing evidence and no premature RCA certainty.
Applied business timing, recurrence, and workaround weakness instead of simplistic downtime-only prioritization.
Rejected thin RCA claims and required evidence-backed hypothesis testing.
Produced known-error guidance with applicability limits, residual risk, and downstream fix ownership.
Handled problem-to-change handoff without crossing into change approval or implementation.
Accepted post-incident follow-up and action tracking while rejecting live major-incident command.
Used trend signals to frame bounded hypotheses and requested stronger correlation evidence.
Clearly routed active outage command work back to incident or major-incident handling.
Refused direct remediation and preserved the specialist boundary while producing a useful handoff.
Handled causal ambiguity with explicit uncertainty, evidence requests, and escalation instead of guessing.
Stayed operational with local baseline and conservative claims when delegated meta-agents were stale or unavailable.
Deployment and commercialization notes remained consistent with boundary, evidence, isolation, and rollback expectations.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the issue #112 requirements, including normal guidance, workflow quality, prioritization, edge handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
